title: Teaching materials
text:
Preface
This is a Quarto book.
To learn more about Quarto books visit https://quarto.org/docs/books.

title: 1Â  Introduction
text:
This is a book created from markdown and executable code.
See Knuth (1984) for additional discussion of literate programming.




Knuth, Donald E. 1984. â€śLiterate Programming.â€ť Comput. J. 27 (2): 97â€“111. https://doi.org/10.1093/comjnl/27.2.97.

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.1 Measures of central tendency and position
Measures of position summarize a dataset using a numerical value. If this numerical value lies toward the center of the distribution, it is referred to as a measure of central position. The main measures of central position are the mean, the median, and the mode.

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.1.1 Arithmetic mean
Given a quantitative statistical variable, \(X\), with values \(x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_k\) and frequencies \(n_1, n_2, \ldots, n_k\), the arithmetic mean is defined, and denoted by \(\bar{x}\), as: \[\bar{x}=\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k n_i x_i\] where \(n\) is the total number of observations.



2.1.2 Median
The median is defined as the value of the statistical variable that divides the dataset into two equal parts, assuming the values are ordered.
Two situations may arise:

If no value \(x_i\) has a cumulative relative frequency equal to 0.5. Then, \(Me = x_i\) such that \(F_{i-1} < 0.5 < F_i\).
If there exists a value \(x_i\) whose cumulative relative frequency is 0.5. In this case, the median is taken as the arithmetic mean of the values \(x_i\) and \(x_{i+1}\), in which case the median will not coincide with any value of the statistical variable. \[Me = \frac{x_{i}+x_{i+1}}{2}\]




2.1.3 Mode
The mode is the value that occurs most frequently. There may be more than one mode.
In the case where the variable has a single mode, it is uniquely defined: \[Mo=x_i\quad \text{if}\quad n_i=\max_j {n_j}\]
When there is more than one mode, statistical software outputs typically display the one with the smallest value.



2.1.4 Quartiles and Percentiles
The percentiles are the values of the statistical variable that divide the dataset (assumed to be ordered in increasing order) into 100 intervals with an equal number of observations. They are denoted by: \(P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_{99}\).
The quartiles divide the dataset into four intervals with an equal number of observations. They are denoted by: \(Q_1, Q_2, Q_3\).

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.2 Measures of dispersion
Measures of dispersion quantify the variability of a set of observations, providing information about the greater or lesser representativeness of the measures of central tendency.

2.2.1 Range
The range, denoted by \(Re\), is defined as the difference between the maximum and minimum values of the distribution. \[Re = \max_i x_i - \min_i x_i\]


2.2.2 Interquartile range
The interquartile range, denoted by \(RIQ\), is defined as the difference between the third and the first quartile. Therefore, it provides information about the range of values in which the central 50% of the observations lie. \[RIQ = Q_3 - Q_1\]


2.2.3 Variance
The variance, denoted by \(\sigma^2\), is defined as the arithmetic mean of the squared deviations between the values of the statistical variable and the arithmetic mean. \[\sigma^2 = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k n_i\left(x_i - \bar{x}\right)^2 =  \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k n_i x_i^2 - \bar{x}^2\]


2.2.4 Standard deviation
The standard deviation, denoted by \(\sigma\), is defined as the positive square root of the variance. 


2.2.5 Coefficient of variation
The Pearson coefficient of variation, denoted by \(CV\), is defined as the ratio between the standard deviation and the arithmetic mean, \[CV =
\frac{\sigma}{\bar{x}}\]
It is used to compare the dispersion of two or more distributions in which the variables are expressed in different units. It represents how many times the standard deviation contains the arithmetic mean; therefore, the larger the coefficient of variation, the greater the number of times the standard deviation contains the arithmetic mean and, consequently, the less representative the arithmetic mean is.

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.3 Measures of shape
Measures of shape describe the silhouette of the distribution.

Skewness coefficient: indicates whether the data cluster more on one side of the mean than the other. It is defined as \[\gamma_1 = \frac{\mu_3}{\sigma_3}\]

If \(\gamma_1 = 0\), the distribution is symmetric. Both tails are equal (as in the Gaussian bell curve).
If \(\gamma_1 > 0\), the distribution is positively skewed (right-skewed). The â€śtailâ€ť of the distribution is longer on the right side.
If \(\gamma_1 < 0\), the distribution is negatively skewed (left-skewed). The â€śtailâ€ť of the distribution is longer on the left side.








Kurtosis coefficient: Measures how â€śpeakedâ€ť or flat the distribution is, i.e., how concentrated the data are in the central region and in the tails (extremes). It is defined as \[\gamma_2 = \frac{\mu_4}{\sigma_4} - 3\]

If \(\gamma_2 = 0\), the distribution is called mesokurtic, as peaked as the normal distribution, i.e., it has normal kurtosis.
If \(\gamma_2 > 0\), the distribution is more peaked than the normal distribution, with many data points concentrated in the center, and it is called leptokurtic.
If \(\gamma_2 < 0\), the distribution is flatter than the normal distribution, with more dispersed data, and it is called platykurtic.

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.4 Graphical representations

Histogram: It is used to visualize the frequency distribution of the variable. This is very useful for detecting asymmetry, identifying kurtosis, and spotting possible outliers.
Boxplot: It consists of graphically representing a summary of the data distribution using its quartiles. It shows the dispersion of the data and is very useful for detecting outliers and asymmetries.

title: 2Â  Descriptive Statistics
text:
2.5 Example: Quality Control in Microelectronics
The thickness of a gold coating layer (in micrometers, \(\mu m\)) deposited on a printed circuit board using a chemical process has been measured. The target thickness is 2.50 \(\mu m\). Here are the data from 15 measurements taken by a high-precision sensor: \[2.42, 2.45, 2.48, 2.50, 2.51, 2.52, 2.53, 2.53, 2.54, 2.56, 2.58, 2.60, 2.62, 2.75, 2.81\] The descriptive statistics obtained in this case are as follows:

2.5.1 Measures of position
Mean: \(\bar{x} = 2.56 ,\mu m\) (higher than the target value of 2.50)
Median: \(Me = 2.53 \mu m\)
Quartiles: \(Q_1 (25\%) = 2.505 \mu m\) y \(Q_3 (75\%) = 2.590 \mu m\)


2.5.2 Measures of dispersion
Range: 2.810 - 2.420 = 0.39\(\mu m\)
Standard deviation: \(\sigma = 0.104\mu m\)
Coefficient of variation: \(0.104/2.56 = 0.041 ,\mu m\). This is a very low dispersion, which indicates a highly precise process, although it is slightly shifted from the target value.


2.5.3 Measures of shape
Skewness coefficient: 1.156 \(\mu m\), indicating right skewness. The Mean (2.56) > Median (2.53).
Kurtosis coefficient: 3.769 \(\mu m\), indicating a sharper peak than normal, i.e., the distribution is leptokurtic. Most thickness values are highly concentrated.


2.5.4 Graphical representations
Histogram and boxplot:





The process shows high precision in 50% of the samples (within the box), but it exhibits instability toward the upper limit. The histogram confirms a positive skew, and the boxplot identifies two outliers at \(2.75 ,\mu m\) and \(2.81 ,\mu m\). It is recommended to recalibrate the process to center the median at \(2.50\) and reduce material waste.

title: 3Â  Regresion and correlation
text:
3.1 Concepto de regresiĂłn
En muchas situaciones prĂˇcticas es frecuente encontrar que existe una cierta dependencia de tipo funcional entre dos variables.
Dada una distribuciĂłn bidimensional, uno de los objetivos que se pueden plantear es el de encontrar una funciĂłn que se ajuste lo mejor posible a las observaciones; grĂˇficamente esto equivale a encontrar una curva que aunque no pase por todos los puntos del diagrama de dispersiĂłn estĂ© lo mĂˇs prĂłxima posible de dichos puntos. Un segundo objetivo serĂˇ medir el grado de ajuste entre la funciĂłn teĂłrica y la nube de puntos.
RegresiĂłn: Consideramos \(n\) observaciones que son pares de valores del tipo \(\left(x_i,y_j\right)\). Si tomamos como variable independiente a \(X\) y como variable dependiente a \(Y\), debemos de hallar una expresiĂłn de \(Y\) como funciĂłn de \(X\). En este caso diremos que estamos realizando una regresiĂłn de \(Y\) sobre \(X\). Si tomamos como variable independiente a \(Y\) y como variable variable dependiente a \(X\), debemos de hallar una expresiĂłn de \(X\) como funciĂłn de \(Y\). En este caso diremos que estamos realizando una regresiĂłn de \(X\) sobre \(Y\).
La funciĂłn de regresiĂłn, \(y = f\left(x\right)\), puede adoptar distintas formas: lĂ­nea recta, parĂˇbola, polinomio de grado n, funciĂłn exponencial, etc. Todas las posibles funciones de ese tipo tendrĂˇn una formulaciĂłn general que dependerĂˇ de unos parĂˇmetros y la determinaciĂłn de dichos parĂˇmetros se harĂˇ de modo que los valores observados estĂ©n prĂłximos a los puntos de la funciĂłn de regresiĂłn. El modo usual de proceder es prefijar el tipo de funciĂłn que se va a considerar.
De modo general, consideremos como variable independiente a \(X\) y como variable dependiente a \(Y\). Si hemos observado un punto \(x_i\), llamamos \(y_j^*\) al valor previsto por la funciĂłn de regresiĂłn, es decir, \(y_j^* = f(x_i)\). Denominaremos error o residuo y lo denotaremos por \(e_{ij}\) a la diferencia entre el valor observado y el valor previsto, es decir: \[e_{ij} = y_j - y_j^*.\] LĂłgicamente se desea que los residuos o errores sean lo mĂˇs pequeĂ±os posible. El mĂ©todo que mĂˇs se utiliza para obtener los parĂˇmetros es el de ajuste por mĂ­nimos cuadrados, que consiste en obtener el valor de los parĂˇmetros que hagan mĂ­nima la suma de los residuos al cuadrado.

title: 3Â  Regresion and correlation
text:
3.2 Covarianza
La covarianza es una medida de la variabilidad conjunta de las variables \(X\) e \(Y\). Se denota como \(\sigma_{xy}\) y cuya expresiĂłn viene dada por \[\sigma_{xy} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^p n_{ij} \left(x_i -
    \bar{x}\right)\left(y_j - \bar{y}\right) = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^p
    n_{ij}x_i y_j - \bar{x}\bar{y}\]
El signo de la covarianza indica el sentido en el que varĂ­an conjuntamente las dos variables:

Si \(\sigma_{xy}>0\), entonces las variables varĂ­an en el mismo sentido.
Si \(\sigma_{xy}<0\), entonces las variables varĂ­an en sentidos opuestos.

title: 3Â  Regresion and correlation
text:
3.3 Recta de regresiĂłn mĂ­nimo-cuadrĂˇtica
Estudiamos el caso mĂˇs sencillo y de mayor importancia, que es aquel en que la funciĂłn de regresiĂłn es una lĂ­nea recta.
Recta de regresiĂłn de \(Y\) sobre \(X\)
La expresiĂłn general de una lĂ­nea recta serĂˇ del tipo \(y = a + bx\), siendo \(a\) y \(b\) los parĂˇmetros desconocidos.
\[\begin{eqnarray}
\nonumber
a = & \bar{y} - b\bar{x}\\\nonumber b = & \frac{\sigma_{xy}}{\sigma_x^2}
\end{eqnarray}\]
Una de las aplicaciones de la recta de regresiĂłn de \(Y\) sobre \(X\) es predecir el valor que tendrĂˇ la variable \(Y\) conocido un valor de \(X\), diferente a los utilizados para construir la recta de regresiĂłn.

title: 3Â  Regresion and correlation
text:
3.4 Coeficiente de correlaciĂłn lineal
Se define el coeficiente de correlaciĂłn lineal de Pearson, y se denota como \(r\) a \[r = \frac{\sigma_{xy}}{\sigma_x \sigma_y};\quad -1\leq r \leq 1,\] que mide el grado de relaciĂłn lineal entre las variables.

Si \(r=0\), no existe relaciĂłn lineal entre las variables.
Si \(r\) estĂˇ prĂłximo a 1 o a -1, entonces existe buena relaciĂłn entre las variables y esa relaciĂłn es directa o inversa, respectivamente.

title: 3Â  Regresion and correlation
section: 3.5 Coeficiente de determinaciĂłn y varianza residual
text:
3.5 Coeficiente de determinaciĂłn y varianza residual
Si consideramos el caso de una regresiĂłn de \(Y\) sobre \(X\), para medir el grado de dependencia funcional entre \(X\) e \(Y\) puede utilizarse el llamado coeficiente de determinaciĂłn, que denotaremos por \(R^2\), y que se calcula como \[R^2 = \frac{\sigma_{y^*}^2}{\sigma_y^2} = 1 - \frac{\frac{1}{n}
\sum_{i=1}^k\sum_{j=1}^p n_{ij} \left(y_j - y_j^*\right)^2}{\sigma_y^2}\] El numerador es la varianza de los valores calculados para cada \(x_i\) mediante la funciĂłn de regresiĂłn y el denominador es la varianza de los valores observados para \(Y\). Se demuestra que \(R^2\) solo puede tomar valores en el intervalo \([0,1]\). Este coeficiente representa la proporciĂłn de la varianza de \(Y\) (variabilidad de la variable) que es explicada por la regresiĂłn.

Si \(R^2 = 1\), indica que existe una dependencia funcional exacta; todos los puntos observados estĂˇn sobre la funciĂłn de regresiĂłn obtenida. En este caso, todos los residuos son nulos y el ajuste es perfecto. Y si \(R^2\) estĂˇ prĂłximo a \(1\) se puede afirmar que el modelo explica la relaciĂłn de dependencia entre las variables.
Si \(R^2 = 0\), entonces el modelo de regresiĂłn considerado no explica nada la variaÂ­biÂ­liÂ­dad de la variable dependiente. Es el peor caso que se puede encontrar. Y si \(R^2\) estĂˇ prĂłximo a 0 no se puede afirmar que el modelo explique la relaciĂłn de dependencia entre las variables.

Todo lo anterior es vĂˇlido para el caso de una funciĂłn de regresiĂłn cualquiera, sin importar la forma que adopte.
En el caso particular de la recta de regresiĂłn se demuestra que: \[R^2 = r^2.\]
Por lo tanto, el coeficiente de correlaciĂłn lineal al cuadrado, \(r^2\), se utiliza como medida de la bondad del ajuste de la recta de mĂ­nimos cuadrados.

title: 6Â  Statistics Application
text:
#| '!! shinylive warning !!': |
#|   shinylive does not work in self-contained HTML documents.
#|   Please set `embed-resources: false` in your metadata.
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 1000

library(shiny)
library(shinydashboard)
library(haven)
library(moments)
library(ggplot2)
library(readxl)
library(shinyjs)
library(BSDA)
# =========================
# FUNCIONES AUXILIARES
# =========================
moda <- function(x){
    ux <- unique(x)
    ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

uploadBox <- function(id){
    ns <- NS(id)
    box(
        title = "Data loading", width = 12, status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE,
        fileInput(ns("file"), "Select file", accept = c(".csv", ".txt", ".xlsx", ".xls", ".sav")),
        selectInput(ns("sep"), "Column separator", c("Comma"=",","Semicolon"=";","Tab"="    ")),
        selectInput(ns("dec"), "Decimal separator", c("Dot"=".","Comma"=",")),
        checkboxInput(ns("header"), "Header", TRUE),
        br(),
        h4("Preview (first 3 rows)"),
        tableOutput(ns("preview"))
    )
}

uploadServer <- function(id){
    moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session){
        
        observe({
            req(input$file)
            ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(input$file$name))
            disable_opts <- ext %in% c("xlsx", "xls", "sav")
            
            shinyjs::toggleState("sep", condition = !disable_opts)
            shinyjs::toggleState("dec", condition = !disable_opts)
            shinyjs::toggleState("header", condition = !disable_opts)
        })
        data_react <- reactive({
            req(input$file)
            ext <- tolower(tools::file_ext(input$file$name))
            
            df <- switch(ext,
                         "sav" = as.data.frame(read_sav(input$file$datapath)),
                         "xlsx" = as.data.frame(read_excel(input$file$datapath)),
                         "xls" = as.data.frame(read_excel(input$file$datapath)),
                         read.table(input$file$datapath,
                                    sep = input$sep,
                                    dec = input$dec,
                                    header = input$header,
                                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                                    check.names = FALSE)
            )
            
            # validate(
            #     need(ncol(df) > 1 || ext %in% c("xlsx","xls","sav"),
            #          "La estructura de los datos no parece correcta. Revisa separadores y cabecera.")
            # )
            
            validate(
                need(ncol(df) > 1 || ext %in% c("xlsx","xls","sav"),
                     "")
            )
            
            as.data.frame(df)
        })
        
        output$preview <- renderTable({
            req(data_react())
            head(data_react(), 3)
        })
        
        return(data_react)
    })
}

# =========================
# UI
# =========================
ui <- dashboardPage(
    dashboardHeader(title = "Click & Learn"),
    dashboardSidebar(
        sidebarMenu(
            menuItem("Descriptive Statistics", tabName = "desc", icon = icon("chart-bar")),
            menuItem("Regression / Correlation", tabName = "reg", icon = icon("chart-line")),
            menuItem("Confidence Intervals", tabName = "ci", icon = icon("percent")),
            menuItem("Hypothesis Testing", tabName = "tests", icon = icon("flask"))
        )
    ),
    dashboardBody(
        useShinyjs(),
        tabItems(
            
            # ========================= DESCRIPTIVAS =========================
            tabItem(tabName = "desc",
                    fluidRow(uploadBox("up_desc")),
                    fluidRow(
                        box(width = 4, title = "Measures", status = "warning", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            uiOutput("vars_desc_ui"),
                            h4("Measures of position"),
                            checkboxInput("mean", "Mean"),
                            checkboxInput("median", "Median"),
                            checkboxInput("mode", "Mode"),
                            checkboxInput("quart", "Quartiles"),
                            textInput("percent", "Percentiles", placeholder = "20,50,80"),
                            br(),
                            h4("Measures of dispersion"),
                            checkboxInput("min", "Minimum"),
                            checkboxInput("max", "Maximum"),
                            checkboxInput("range", "Range"),
                            checkboxInput("iqr", "Interquartile range"),
                            checkboxInput("var", "Variance"),
                            checkboxInput("sd", "Standard deviation"),
                            checkboxInput("cv", "Coefficient of variation"),
                            br(),
                            h4("Measures of Shape"),
                            checkboxInput("skew", "Skewness"),
                            checkboxInput("kurt", "Kurtosis"),
                            br(),
                            h4("Graphical representations"),
                            checkboxInput("show_hist", "Histogram"),
                            checkboxInput("normal", "Overlay Normal curve"),
                            checkboxInput("show_box", "Boxplot")
                        ),
                        box(width = 8, title = "Results", status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            tableOutput("tabla_desc"),
                            br(),
                            uiOutput("plots_desc")
                        )
                    )
            ),
            
            # ========================= REGRESIĂ“N =========================
            tabItem(tabName = "reg",
                    fluidRow(uploadBox("up_reg")),
                    fluidRow(
                        box(width = 4, title = "Regression Fit", status = "warning", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            
                            uiOutput("dep_ui"),
                            uiOutput("indep_ui"),
                            
                            br(),
                            h4("Model"),
                            checkboxInput("corr_matrix", "Correlation matrix"),
                            checkboxInput("corr_test", "Correlation test"),
                            checkboxInput("cov_matrix", "Variance-Covariance matrix"),
                            checkboxInput("r2_show", "Coefficient of determination (RÂ˛)"),
                            checkboxInput("show_scatter", "Scatterplot + fit"),
                            
                            br(),
                            h4("Normality Tests"),
                            checkboxInput("do_shapiro", "Shapiro-Wilk test"),
                            checkboxInput("do_qq", "Q-Q plot")
                        ),
                        box(width = 8, title = "Results", status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            tableOutput("reg_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("r2_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("corr_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("cov_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("corr_test_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("shapiro_table"),
                            br(),
                            uiOutput("qq_ui"),
                            br(),
                            plotOutput("scatter_plot")
                        )
                    )
            ),
            
            # ========================= IC =========================
            tabItem(tabName = "ci",
                    fluidRow(uploadBox("up_ci")),
                    fluidRow(
                        box(width = 4, title = "Confidence Intervals", status = "warning", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            
                            uiOutput("vars_ci_ui"),
                            uiOutput("group_var_ui"),
                            
                            br(),
                            #h4("Intervalos de confianza"),
                            
                            checkboxInput("ci_mean", "Mean CI"),
                            checkboxInput("ci_var", "Variance CI"),
                            checkboxInput("ci_diff", "Means difference CI"),
                            checkboxInput("ci_ratio_var", "Variances ratio CI"),
                            
                            numericInput("conf_level", "Confidence level (%)", 95, min = 1, max = 99.9)
                        ),
                        box(width = 8, title = "Results", status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            tableOutput("ci_meanvar_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("ci_diff_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("ci_ratio_table")
                        )
                    )
            ),
            
            # ========================= TESTS =========================
            tabItem(tabName = "tests",
                    fluidRow(uploadBox("up_test")),
                    fluidRow(
                        box(width = 4, title = "Hypothesis tests", status = "warning", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            
                            uiOutput("vars_test_ui"),
                            uiOutput("group_test_ui"),
                            
                            br(),
                            h4("Normality Tests"),
                            checkboxInput("test_shapiro", "Shapiro-Wilk test"),
                            checkboxInput("test_qq", "Q-Q plot"),
                            
                            br(),
                            h4("Parametric Tests"),
                            
                            textInput("mu_test", "Hypothesized mean", placeholder = "10"),
                            textInput("var_test", "Hypothesized variance", placeholder = "5"),
                            
                            checkboxInput("t1", "One-sample t-test"),
                            checkboxInput("var1", "Variance test"),
                            checkboxInput("var_ratio", "Variances ratio test"),
                            checkboxInput("t_indep", "Independent samples t-test"),
                            checkboxInput("welch", "Welch test"),
                            
                            br(),
                            h4("Non-parametric Tests"),
                            
                            textInput("median_test", "Hypothesized median", placeholder = "10"),
                            
                            checkboxInput("sign_test", "Sign test"),
                            checkboxInput("mw", "Mann-Whitney test"),
                            checkboxInput("ks", "Kolmogorov-Smirnov test")
                        ),
                        box(width = 8, title = "Results", status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE,
                            
                            tableOutput("param_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("var_ratio_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("t_indep_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("welch_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("sign_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("mw_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("ks_table"),
                            br(),
                            tableOutput("shapiro_test_table"),
                            br(),
                            uiOutput("qq_test_ui")
                        )
                    )
            )
        )
    )
)

# ========================= SERVER
# =========================
server <- function(input, output, session){
    
    data_desc <- uploadServer("up_desc")
    data_reg  <- uploadServer("up_reg")
    data_ci   <- uploadServer("up_ci")
    data_test <- uploadServer("up_test")
    
    # ---------- DESCRIPTIVAS ----------
    output$vars_desc_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_desc())
        req(data_desc())
        nums <- names(data_desc())[sapply(data_desc(), is.numeric)]
        selectInput("vars_desc", "Variables", nums, multiple = TRUE)
    })
    
    observe({
        if(isTRUE(input$show_hist)){
            shinyjs::enable("normal")
        } else {
            shinyjs::disable("normal")
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "normal", value = FALSE)
        }
    })
    
    output$tabla_desc <- renderTable({
        req(input$vars_desc)
        df <- data_desc()
        res <- list()
        percentiles <- if(nchar(input$percent)>0) as.numeric(strsplit(input$percent,",")[[1]]) else NULL
        
        for(v in input$vars_desc){
            x <- na.omit(df[[v]])
            tmp <- c()
            if(input$mean) tmp['Mean'] <- mean(x)
            if(input$median) tmp['Median'] <- median(x)
            if(input$mode) tmp['Mode'] <- moda(x)
            if(input$quart){
                q <- quantile(x, c(.25,.5,.75))
                tmp['Q1'] <- q[1]; tmp['Q2'] <- q[2]; tmp['Q3'] <- q[3]
            }
            if(!is.null(percentiles)){
                q <- quantile(x, percentiles/100)
                for(i in seq_along(q)) tmp[paste0('P', percentiles[i])] <- q[i]
            }
            if(input$min) tmp['Minimum'] <- min(x)
            if(input$max) tmp['Maximum'] <- max(x)
            if(input$range) tmp['Range'] <- max(x) - min(x)
            if(input$iqr) tmp['Interquartile range'] <- IQR(x)
            if(input$var) tmp['Variance'] <- var(x)
            if(input$sd) tmp['SD'] <- sd(x)
            if(input$cv) tmp['Coeff. variation'] <- sd(x)/mean(x)
            if(input$skew) tmp['Skewness'] <- skewness(x)
            if(input$kurt) tmp['Kurtosis'] <- kurtosis(x)
            res[[v]] <- tmp
        }
        as.data.frame(res)
    }, rownames = TRUE)
    
    output$plots_desc <- renderUI({
        req(input$vars_desc)
        
        graficos <- list()
        
        for(v in input$vars_desc){
            
            if(input$show_hist){
                graficos <- append(graficos, list(
                    plotOutput(paste0("hist_", v), height = 250)
                ))
            }
            
            if(input$show_box){
                graficos <- append(graficos, list(
                    plotOutput(paste0("box_", v), height = 250)
                ))
            }
        }
        
        graficos
    })
    
    observe({
        req(input$vars_desc)
        
        df <- data_desc()
        
        for(v in input$vars_desc){
            
            local({
                varname <- v
                
                output[[paste0("hist_", varname)]] <- renderPlot({
                    
                    req(input$show_hist)
                    
                    x <- na.omit(df[[varname]])
                    
                    hist(
                        x,
                        probability = TRUE,
                        main = paste("Histogram", varname),
                        col = "gray80"
                    )
                    
                    if(input$normal){
                        curve(
                            dnorm(x, mean(x), sd(x)),
                            add = TRUE,
                            col = "red",
                            lwd = 2
                        )
                    }
                })
                
                output[[paste0("box_", varname)]] <- renderPlot({
                    
                    req(input$show_box)
                    
                    x <- na.omit(df[[varname]])
                    
                    boxplot(
                        x,
                        horizontal = FALSE,
                        main = paste("Boxplot", varname)
                    )
                })
            })
        }
    })
    
    # ---------- REGRESIĂ“N ----------
    output$dep_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_reg())
        
        nums <- names(data_reg())[sapply(data_reg(), is.numeric)]
        
        selectInput(
            "dep_var",
            "Dependent variable",
            choices = c("Select variable" = "", nums),
            selected = ""
        )
    })
    
    output$indep_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_reg(), input$dep_var)
        
        nums <- names(data_reg())[sapply(data_reg(), is.numeric)]
        nums <- nums[nums != input$dep_var]
        
        selectInput(
            "indep_vars",
            "Independent variable",
            nums,
            multiple = TRUE
        )
    })
    
    observe({
        if(length(input$indep_vars) == 1){
            shinyjs::enable("show_scatter")
        } else {
            shinyjs::disable("show_scatter")
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "show_scatter", value = FALSE)
        }
    })
    
    output$reg_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$dep_var != "", input$indep_vars)
        
        fit <- lm(
            as.formula(
                paste(input$dep_var, "~", paste(input$indep_vars, collapse = "+"))
            ),
            data = data_reg()
        )
        
        coefs <- as.data.frame(summary(fit)$coefficients)
        
        coefs$Coeficiente <- rownames(coefs)
        rownames(coefs) <- NULL
        
        coefs <- coefs[, c(
            "Coeficiente",
            "Estimate",
            "Std. Error",
            "t value",
            "Pr(>|t|)"
        )]
        
        names(coefs) <- c(
            "Coefficient",
            "Estimate",
            "Std. error",
            "t",
            "p_value"
        )
        
        coefs[, -1] <- round(coefs[, -1], 5)
        
        coefs
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    output$corr_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$corr_matrix, input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        round(
            cor(data_reg()[vars], use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
            5
        )
        
    }, rownames = TRUE)
    
    output$cov_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$cov_matrix, input$dep_var != "", input$indep_vars)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        round(
            cov(data_reg()[vars], use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
            5
        )
        
    }, rownames = TRUE)
    
    output$r2_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$r2_show, input$dep_var != "", input$indep_vars)
        
        fit <- lm(
            as.formula(
                paste(input$dep_var, "~", paste(input$indep_vars, collapse = "+"))
            ),
            data = data_reg()
        )
        
        data.frame(
            Measure = c("RÂ˛", "RÂ˛ fitted"),
            Value = round(
                c(summary(fit)$r.squared,
                  summary(fit)$adj.r.squared),
                5
            )
        )
        
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    output$corr_test_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$corr_test, input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        if(length(vars) < 2) return(NULL)
        
        cmb <- combn(vars, 2, simplify = FALSE)
        
        do.call(rbind, lapply(cmb, function(p){
            
            ct <- cor.test(
                data_reg()[[p[1]]],
                data_reg()[[p[2]]]
            )
            
            data.frame(
                Variable_1 = p[1],
                Variable_2 = p[2],
                Correlation = round(ct$estimate, 5),
                p_value = round(ct$p.value, 5)
            )
        }))
        
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    output$shapiro_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$do_shapiro, input$dep_var)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        do.call(rbind, lapply(vars, function(v){
            
            x <- na.omit(data_reg()[[v]])
            
            if(length(x) < 3 || length(x) > 5000){
                return(data.frame(
                    Variable = v,
                    W = NA,
                    p_value = NA
                ))
            }
            
            sh <- shapiro.test(x)
            
            data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                W = round(sh$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(sh$p.value, 5)
            )
        }))
        
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    output$qq_ui <- renderUI({
        req(input$do_qq, input$dep_var)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        lapply(vars, function(v){
            plotOutput(paste0("qq_", v), height = 250)
        })
    })
    
    observe({
        req(input$do_qq, input$dep_var)
        
        vars <- c(input$dep_var, input$indep_vars)
        
        for(v in vars){
            local({
                varname <- v
                
                output[[paste0("qq_", varname)]] <- renderPlot({
                    x <- na.omit(data_reg()[[varname]])
                    
                    qqnorm(x, main = paste("Q-Q plot:", varname))
                    qqline(x, col = "red", lwd = 2)
                })
            })
        }
    })
    
    output$scatter_plot <- renderPlot({
        req(input$show_scatter)
        req(input$dep_var)
        
        if(length(input$indep_vars) != 1) return(NULL)
        
        ggplot(
            data_reg(),
            aes_string(
                x = input$indep_vars[1],
                y = input$dep_var
            )
        ) +
            geom_point() +
            geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
            theme_minimal()
    })
    
    # ---------- IC ----------
    output$vars_ci_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_ci())
        nums <- names(data_ci())[sapply(data_ci(), is.numeric)]
        selectInput("vars_ci", "Variables", nums, multiple = TRUE)
    })
    
    output$group_var_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_ci())
        
        df <- data_ci()
        
        # variables NO numĂ©ricas â†’ factores
        facs <- names(df)[!sapply(df, is.numeric)]
        
        if(length(facs) == 0) return(NULL)
        
        selectInput(
            "group_var",
            "Grouping variable",
            choices = c("None" = "", facs),
            selected = ""
        )
    })
    
    observe({
        
        # nĂşmero de variables numĂ©ricas seleccionadas
        n_vars <- length(input$vars_ci)
        
        # si no hay agrupaciĂłn
        no_group <- is.null(input$group_var) || input$group_var == ""
        
        # IC diferencia medias
        if(n_vars < 1 || no_group){
            shinyjs::disable("ci_diff")
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "ci_diff", value = FALSE)
        } else {
            shinyjs::enable("ci_diff")
        }
        
        # IC cociente varianzas
        if(n_vars < 1 || no_group){
            shinyjs::disable("ci_ratio_var")
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "ci_ratio_var", value = FALSE)
        } else {
            shinyjs::enable("ci_ratio_var")
        }
    })
    
    output$ci_meanvar_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$vars_ci)
        
        conf <- ifelse(is.null(input$conf_level), 0.95, input$conf_level/100)
        alpha <- 1 - conf
        
        res <- list()
        
        for(v in input$vars_ci){
            x <- na.omit(data_ci()[[v]])
            n <- length(x)
            m <- mean(x)
            s2 <- var(x)
            
            fila <- data.frame(Variable = v)
            
            if(input$ci_mean){
                tval <- qt(1 - alpha/2, n - 1)
                li <- m - tval * sqrt(s2/n)
                ls <- m + tval * sqrt(s2/n)
                
                fila$Mean <- round(m,5)
                fila$LowerL_Mean <- round(li,5)
                fila$UpperL_Mean <- round(ls,5)
            }
            
            if(input$ci_var){
                chi1 <- qchisq(alpha/2, n-1)
                chi2 <- qchisq(1 - alpha/2, n-1)
                
                li <- (n-1)*s2/chi2
                ls <- (n-1)*s2/chi1
                
                fila$Variance <- round(s2,5)
                fila$LowerL_Var <- round(li,5)
                fila$UpperL_Var <- round(ls,5)
            }
            
            res[[v]] <- fila
        }
        
        do.call(rbind, res)
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    
    output$ci_diff_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$ci_diff, input$vars_ci, input$group_var)
        
        df <- data_ci()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_var]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        conf <- input$conf_level/100
        alpha <- 1 - conf
        
        res <- list()
        
        for(v in input$vars_ci){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            n1 <- length(x1); n2 <- length(x2)
            m1 <- mean(x1); m2 <- mean(x2)
            s1 <- var(x1); s2 <- var(x2)
            
            sp <- ((n1-1)*s1 + (n2-1)*s2)/(n1+n2-2)
            se <- sqrt(sp*(1/n1 + 1/n2))
            
            tval <- qt(1 - alpha/2, n1+n2-2)
            
            diff <- m1 - m2
            
            li <- diff - tval * se
            ls <- diff + tval * se
            
            res[[v]] <- data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Mean_Group1 = round(m1,5),
                Mean_Group2 = round(m2,5),
                Difference = round(diff,5),
                LowerL = round(li,5),
                UpperL = round(ls,5)
            )
        }
        
        do.call(rbind, res)
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    
    output$ci_ratio_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$ci_ratio_var, input$vars_ci, input$group_var)
        
        df <- data_ci()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_var]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        conf <- input$conf_level/100
        alpha <- 1 - conf
        
        res <- list()
        
        for(v in input$vars_ci){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            n1 <- length(x1); n2 <- length(x2)
            s1 <- var(x1); s2 <- var(x2)
            
            ratio <- s1/s2
            
            f1 <- qf(alpha/2, n1-1, n2-1)
            f2 <- qf(1 - alpha/2, n1-1, n2-1)
            
            li <- ratio / f2
            ls <- ratio / f1
            
            res[[v]] <- data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Var_Group1 = round(s1,5),
                Var_Group2 = round(s2,5),
                Ratio = round(ratio,5),
                LowerL = round(li,5),
                UpperL = round(ls,5)
            )
        }
        
        do.call(rbind, res)
    }, rownames = FALSE)
    
    
    # ---------- TESTS ----------
    output$vars_test_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_test())
        nums <- names(data_test())[sapply(data_test(), is.numeric)]
        selectInput("vars_test", "Variables", nums, multiple = TRUE)
    })
    
    output$group_test_ui <- renderUI({
        req(data_test())
        
        df <- data_test()
        facs <- names(df)[!sapply(df, is.numeric)]
        
        if(length(facs) == 0) return(NULL)
        
        selectInput(
            "group_test",
            "Grouping variable",
            choices = c("None" = "", facs),
            selected = ""
        )
    })
    
    observe({
        
        n_vars <- length(input$vars_test)
        no_group <- is.null(input$group_test) || input$group_test == ""
        
        # ParamĂ©tricos con grupos
        if(n_vars < 1 || no_group){
            shinyjs::disable("var_ratio")
            shinyjs::disable("t_indep")
            shinyjs::disable("welch")
            
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "var_ratio", value = FALSE)
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "t_indep", value = FALSE)
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "welch", value = FALSE)
        } else {
            shinyjs::enable("var_ratio")
            shinyjs::enable("t_indep")
            shinyjs::enable("welch")
        }
        
        # No paramĂ©trico MW
        if(n_vars < 1 || no_group){
            shinyjs::disable("mw")
            updateCheckboxInput(session, "mw", value = FALSE)
        } else {
            shinyjs::enable("mw")
        }
    })
    
    output$param_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$vars_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x <- na.omit(df[[v]])
            
            # --- t una muestra ---
            if(input$t1){
                tt <- t.test(x, mu = as.numeric(input$mu_test))
                
                out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                    Variable = v,
                    Test = "One-sample t-test",
                    Statistic = round(tt$statistic, 5),
                    p_value = round(tt$p.value, 5)
                ))
            }
            
            # --- test varianza ---
            if(input$var1){
                n <- length(x)
                s2 <- var(x)
                chi <- (n-1)*s2/as.numeric(input$var_test)
                
                pval <- 2 * min(
                    pchisq(chi, df = n-1),
                    1 - pchisq(chi, df = n-1)
                )
                
                out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                    Variable = v,
                    Test = "Variance test",
                    Statistic = round(chi, 5),
                    p_value = round(pval, 5)
                ))
            }
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$var_ratio_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$var_ratio, input$vars_test, input$group_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_test]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            ft <- var.test(x1, x2)
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Variances ratio test",
                Statistic = round(ft$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(ft$p.value, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$t_indep_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$t_indep, input$vars_test, input$group_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_test]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            tt <- t.test(x1, x2, var.equal = TRUE)
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Independent samples test",
                Statistic = round(tt$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(tt$p.value, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$welch_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$welch, input$vars_test, input$group_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_test]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            tt <- t.test(x1, x2, var.equal = FALSE)
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Welch test",
                Statistic = round(tt$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(tt$p.value, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$sign_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$sign_test, input$vars_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            # x <- na.omit(df[[v]])
            # 
            # signs <- sum(x > input$median_test)
            # n <- length(x)
            # 
            # pval <- binom.test(signs, n, 0.5)$p.value
            
            x <- na.omit(df[[v]])
            
            signs <- sum(x > input$median_test)
            n <- length(x)
            
            pval <- SIGN.test(x, md = as.numeric(input$median_test))$p.value
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Sign test",
                Statistic = signs,
                p_value = round(pval, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$mw_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$mw, input$vars_test, input$group_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        g <- as.factor(df[[input$group_test]])
        
        if(length(unique(g)) != 2) return(NULL)
        
        niveles <- levels(g)
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x1 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[1], v])
            x2 <- na.omit(df[g == niveles[2], v])
            
            mw <- wilcox.test(x1, x2)
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Mann-Whitney test",
                Statistic = round(mw$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(mw$p.value, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$ks_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$ks, input$vars_test)
        
        df <- data_test()
        out <- data.frame()
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            
            x <- na.omit(df[[v]])
            
            ks <- ks.test(x, "pnorm", mean(x), sd(x))
            
            out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                Test = "Kolmogorov-Smirnov test",
                Statistic = round(ks$statistic, 5),
                p_value = round(ks$p.value, 5)
            ))
        }
        
        out
    })
    
    output$shapiro_test_table <- renderTable({
        req(input$test_shapiro, input$vars_test)
        
        do.call(rbind, lapply(input$vars_test, function(v){
            x <- na.omit(data_test()[[v]])
            sh <- shapiro.test(x)
            
            data.frame(
                Variable = v,
                W = round(sh$statistic,5),
                p_value = round(sh$p.value,5)
            )
        }))
    })
    
    output$qq_test_ui <- renderUI({
        req(input$test_qq, input$vars_test)
        
        lapply(input$vars_test, function(v){
            plotOutput(paste0("qq_test_", v))
        })
    })
    
    observe({
        req(input$test_qq, input$vars_test)
        
        for(v in input$vars_test){
            local({
                varname <- v
                output[[paste0("qq_test_", varname)]] <- renderPlot({
                    x <- na.omit(data_test()[[varname]])
                    qqnorm(x)
                    qqline(x, col="red")
                })
            })
        }
    })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

title: 10Â  Reliability application
11Â  Redundancy application
text:
#| '!! shinylive warning !!': |
#|   shinylive does not work in self-contained HTML documents.
#|   Please set `embed-resources: false` in your metadata.
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 1000


library(shiny)
library(shinydashboard)

# Interfaz de usuario
ui <- dashboardPage(
    dashboardHeader(title = "Redundancy"),
    
    dashboardSidebar(
        sidebarMenu(
            menuItem("Input Parameters", tabName = "input_tab", icon = icon("edit")),
            menuItem("Results", tabName = "results_tab", icon = icon("chart-bar"))
        )
    ),
    
    dashboardBody(
        tabItems(
            # PestaĂ±a de parĂˇmetros de entrada
            tabItem(
                tabName = "input_tab",
                h2("Mathematical Explanation and Input Parameters", style = "text-align: center;"),
                fluidRow(
                    box(
                        title = "Mathematical Explanation", status = "info", solidHeader = TRUE, width = 6,
                        withMathJax(
                            p("Reliability is the science of predicting, estimating and 
                              optimizing the probability of survival, the average life, or in 
                              general, the distribution of the lifetime of a system. A system 
                              or structure is understood as an ordered configuration of 
                              components intended to fulfill a certain mission, that is, a 
                              series of blocks interconnected in such a way that they perform 
                              a set of required functions."),
                            
                            p("Let \\(X=(X_{1},X_{2},\\ldots,X_{n})\\) be the state vector 
                              formed by the state of all the components, with \\( n \\) being 
                              the size of the system. The system structure function 
                              \\(\\phi(X)\\) is defined as the function that indicates the 
                              state of the system based on the state of its components, 
                              taking the value 1 if the system is operative and 0 otherwise."),
                            
                            p("The reliability function is defined as the probability that 
                              the system is operative, i.e.,$$R=P[\\phi (X)=1]=E[\\phi(X)].$$"),
                            
                            p("In case of having independent system components, the 
                              reliability of the system depends on the reliability of the 
                              components, i.e.,$$R=R(p),$$"),
                            
                            p("where \\(p=(p_{1},p_{2},\\ldots,p_{n})\\) is a vector 
                              containing the reliability of each of the system components."),
                            
                            p("The structure and reliability functions of the series system 
                              are defined as 
                              $$\\phi(X)=\\prod_{i=1}^{n}X_{i}, \\quad \\quad \\quad R(p)=\\prod_{i=1}^{n}p_{i},$$"),
                            
                            p("and the parallel system by the following expressions
                              $$\\phi(X)=1-\\prod_{i=1}^{n}(1-X_{i}), \\quad \\quad \\quad R(p)=1-\\prod_{i=1}^{n}(1-p_{i}).$$"),
                            
                            p("In this problem we consider a system formed by \\( n \\) 
                              blocks arranged in series of components, for which we know 
                              their reliability. Our goal is to add the minimum number of 
                              components in parallel to each block with the same 
                              reliability, until we find the optimal configuration that 
                              increases the system reliability above a desired value.")
                        )
                    ),
                    box(
                        title = "Input Parameters", status = "primary", solidHeader = TRUE, width = 6,
                        textInput("p_values", "Reliability components (e.g., 0.9, 0.8, 0.85):", value = "0.9, 0.8, 0.85"),
                        sliderInput("R_limit", "System limit reliability:", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.95, step = 0.01),
                        actionButton("calc_button", "Calculate"),
                        tags$div(textOutput("error_message"), style = "color: red; margin-top: 10px;")
                    )
                )
            ),
            
            # PestaĂ±a de resultados
            tabItem(
                tabName = "results_tab",
                h2("Results", style = "text-align: center;"),
                
                # Caja para resultados de fiabilidad
                fluidRow(
                    box(
                        title = "Reliability Results", status = "info", solidHeader = TRUE, width = 12,
                        textOutput("R_ini_output"),
                        textOutput("R_optim_output"),
                        textOutput("n_replicas_output")
                    )
                ),
                
                # Caja para la tabla de fiabilidad
                fluidRow(
                    box(
                        title = "Reliability Table", status = "warning", solidHeader = TRUE, width = 12,
                        uiOutput("table_ui")
                    )
                ),
                
                # Caja para el grĂˇfico de fiabilidad
                fluidRow(
                    box(
                        title = "Reliability Evolution Plot", status = "success", solidHeader = TRUE, width = 12,
                        plotOutput("reliability_plot")
                    )
                )
            )
        )
    )
)

# Servidor
server <- function(input, output) {
    observeEvent(input$calc_button, {
        if (trimws(input$p_values) == "") {
            output$error_message <- renderText("Error: Please enter reliability components in the field.")
            return()
        }
        
        p <- as.numeric(unlist(strsplit(input$p_values, ",")))
        R_limit <- input$R_limit
        
        if (all(!is.na(p)) && all(p >= 0 & p <= 1) && R_limit >= 0 && R_limit <= 1) {
            result <- Redundance(p, R_limit)
            
            output$R_ini_output <- renderText({ paste("Initial reliability:", format(result$R.ini, digits = 5, nsmall = 5)) })
            output$R_optim_output <- renderText({ paste("Optimized reliability:", format(result$R.optim[length(result$R.optim)], digits = 5, nsmall = 5)) })
            output$n_replicas_output <- renderText({ paste("Number of replicas:", (result$n.replicas) - 1) })
            
            output$table_ui <- renderUI({
                table <- renderTable({
                    mat <- as.data.frame(result$mat.optim)
                    colnames(mat) <- paste0("Component ", 1:ncol(mat))
                    mat$`System reliability` <- format(result$R.optim, digits = 5, nsmall = 5)
                    rownames(mat) <- c("Initial reliabilities", paste0("Replica ", 1:(nrow(mat) - 1)))
                    mat
                }, rownames = TRUE, colnames = TRUE, align = "c")
                tags$div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center; text-align: center;", table)
            })
            
            output$reliability_plot <- renderPlot({
                plot(1:length(result$R.optim), result$R.optim, type = "o", xlab = "Number of replicas",
                     ylab = "System reliability", main = "Evolution of reliability with redundancy", col = "blue", pch = 19)
                abline(h = R_limit, col = "red", lty = 2)
                legend("bottomright", legend = c("Reliability", "Limit"), col = c("blue", "red"), lty = c(1, 2), pch = c(19, NA))
            })
            
            output$error_message <- renderText("")
        } else {
            output$error_message <- renderText("Error: Enter valid p values in [0, 1] and ensure the reliability limit is within [0, 1].")
        }
    })
}

# Funciones auxiliares
s.p <- function(mat.p) {
    n.col <- dim(mat.p)[2]
    R.col <- double(n.col)
    for (i in 1:n.col) {
        R.col[i] <- (1 - prod(1 - mat.p[, i]))
    }
    R <- prod(R.col)
    R
}

Redundance <- function(p, R.limit) {
    n <- length(p)
    mat.optim <- matrix(p, 1, n)
    
    R.ini <- s.p(mat.optim)
    R.optim <- c(R.ini)
    k <- 1
    
    while (R.optim[k] < R.limit) {
        R.aux <- double(n)
        k <- k + 1
        for (i in 1:n) {
            mat.aux <- rbind(mat.optim, rep(0, n))
            mat.aux[k, i] <- p[i]
            R.aux[i] <- s.p(mat.aux)
        }
        i.optim <- which.max(R.aux)
        R.optim <- c(R.optim, R.aux[i.optim])
        p.optim <- double(n); p.optim[i.optim] <- p[i.optim]
        mat.optim <- rbind(mat.optim, p.optim)
    }
    
    return(list(R.ini = R.ini, R.optim = R.optim, mat.optim = mat.optim, n.replicas = k))
}

# Ejecutar aplicaciĂłn
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
